rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings only.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, validate that all referenced identifiers resolve to valid AADL features or declared constants/variables.
Use only visible identifiers from the current component scope in AGREE annexes to prevent resolution errors.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations match supported types: int, real, bool.
For implementation-level signal routing, use assign statements for output features or declared auxiliary variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings.
Helper declarations (eq, const) must reference valid types and identifiers within the same annex.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE reference errors; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid placing guarantee expressions in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings.
